company.name: TaxBridge
company.goal: Build TaxBridge MVP — a US-Canada Cross-Border Tax Bridge web app for the core scenario: H-1B/TN visa tech workers with US RSUs who now live in Canada. MVP features: (1) RSU vesting entry with date, FMV, shares, employer (Meta/Amazon/Google/Microsoft), (2) Dual-country tax calculator showing US federal+state and Canada federal+provincial tax on RSU income using treaty Article XV, (3) Foreign Tax Credit optimizer to avoid double taxation, (4) Required forms checklist (W-2, 1040/1040-NR, T1, T4, FBAR, 8938, 8833), (5) USD/CAD conversion at Bank of Canada rates, (6) Dashboard with filing status. Tech: Next.js App Router, TailwindCSS, TypeScript, SQLite via better-sqlite3. Modern fintech UI.

tasks.title: Initialize Next.js 14+ App Router project with shadcn/ui and fintech-grade styling
tasks.description: 1. Run `npx create-next-app@latest cross-border-tax --typescript --tailwind --app --eslint --src-dir=false --import-alias='@/*'` (select defaults for other prompts). 2. Install shadcn/ui: `npx shadcn-ui@latest init` (select New York style, Slate base color, CSS variables=yes). 3. Create `/app/layout.tsx` with: RootLayout component, Inter font from next/font/google, dark mode support via `<html className="dark">`, metadata export with title='TaxBridge - US-Canada Cross-Border Tax Calculator', viewport config. 4. Create `/app/page.tsx` with: Hero section ('Simplify Your Cross-Border Tax Filing'), feature cards (RSU Calculator, Tax Optimizer, Forms Checklist), CTA button linking to /dashboard, responsive grid layout (Tailwind). 5. Create `/app/globals.css` with: shadcn/ui CSS variables, dark mode color scheme (slate-900 bg, emerald-500 primary, amber-500 secondary for fintech feel), custom utility classes. 6. Install additional deps: `npm install clsx tailwind-merge lucide-react`. Acceptance: `npm run dev` serves app on :3000, landing page renders with dark theme, no TypeScript errors, ESLint passes, responsive on mobile/desktop.

tasks.title: Implement SQLite database with better-sqlite3, schema migrations, and type-safe queries
tasks.description: 1. Install: `npm install better-sqlite3 && npm install -D @types/better-sqlite3`. 2. Create `/lib/db/schema.sql` with DDL: `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS rsu_entries (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, user_id INTEGER NOT NULL, vest_date TEXT NOT NULL, fmv_usd REAL NOT NULL, shares INTEGER NOT NULL, employer TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(employer IN ('Meta','Amazon','Google','Microsoft')), created_at TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);` and similar for `tax_calculations` (with FOREIGN KEY to rsu_entries), `user_profiles` (with province/state TEXT fields, filing_status TEXT). Add indexes on user_id, vest_date. 3. Create `/lib/db/index.ts` with: (a) `getDatabase()` singleton returning Database instance from better-sqlite3, (b) `initializeDatabase()` function reading schema.sql and executing via db.exec(), (c) `runMigrations()` placeholder for future schema versions, (d) export prepared statement helpers `insertRSUEntry()`, `getTaxCalculations()`. 4. Create `/lib/db/seed.ts` with sample data (3-5 RSU entries for testing). 5. Create `/scripts/init-db.ts` that calls initializeDatabase() and optionally seeds data. 6. Add npm script: `"db:init": "tsx scripts/init-db.ts"`. 7. Create `/data/.gitkeep` (add /data/*.db to .gitignore). Acceptance: Run `npm run db:init`, verify `/data/taxbridge.db` created, use sqlite3 CLI to verify tables/indexes exist, TypeScript compilation has no errors.

tasks.title: Define TypeScript domain models and 2025 US/Canada tax bracket constants with CRA/IRS sources
tasks.description: 1. Create `/lib/types.ts` with: `export interface RSUEntry { id?: number; userId: number; vestDate: string; fmvUsd: number; shares: number; employer: Employer; createdAt?: string; }`, `export interface TaxCalculation { id?: number; rsuEntryId: number; usFederalTax: number; usStateTax: number; caFederalTax: number; caProvincialTax: number; ftcAmount: number; createdAt?: string; }`, `export interface UserProfile { id?: number; name: string; province: Province; state: State | null; filingStatus: FilingStatus; }`, `export enum Employer { Meta = 'Meta', Amazon = 'Amazon', Google = 'Google', Microsoft = 'Microsoft' }`, `export enum Province { BC = 'BC', ON = 'ON', AB = 'AB', QC = 'QC' }`, `export enum State { WA = 'WA', CA = 'CA', NY = 'NY', TX = 'TX', NONE = 'NONE' }`, `export enum FilingStatus { Single = 'Single', MarriedFilingJointly = 'MarriedFilingJointly', MarriedFilingSeparately = 'MarriedFilingSeparately', HeadOfHousehold = 'HeadOfHousehold' }`. 2. Create `/lib/constants/tax-brackets.ts` with: (a) `US_FEDERAL_2025` object with brackets array `[{min: 0, max: 11925, rate: 0.10}, {min: 11925, max: 48475, rate: 0.12}, ...]` for Single/MFJ per IRS Rev. Proc. 2024-40, (b) `US_STATE_2025` object keyed by state (WA: {rate: 0, brackets: []}, CA: {brackets: [...]}, NY, TX), (c) `CA_FEDERAL_2025` with CRA 2025 brackets (15% up to $55,867, 20.5% up to $111,733, etc.), (d) `CA_PROVINCIAL_2025` keyed by province (BC, ON rates), (e) `STANDARD_DEDUCTION_2025` and `BASIC_PERSONAL_AMOUNT_2025` constants. 3. Add JSDoc comments with source URLs (irs.gov, canada.ca/cra). 4. Create `/lib/currency.ts` with `convertUsdToCad(amount: number, rate: number): number` helper. Acceptance: All types compile with `npm run build`, no eslint errors, tax brackets match published 2025 IRS/CRA documents (verify spot-check: US Single $100k income = $17,400 + 24% of excess over $100,525).

tasks.title: Build RSU Entry Form with shadcn/ui Components
tasks.description:
**Prerequisites:** Run `npx create-next-app@latest . --typescript --tailwind --app --src-dir false --import-alias '@/*'` if package.json doesn't exist. Install dependencies: `npm install zod react-hook-form @hookform/resolvers date-fns lucide-react`. Install shadcn/ui: `npx shadcn@latest init` (choose default config), then `npx shadcn@latest add form input button calendar select card label`.

**Files to create:**
- `/components/rsu/rsu-entry-form.tsx` - Main form component using shadcn/ui Form with react-hook-form. Import RSUEventSchema from `/lib/types.ts`. Fields: vestingDate (Calendar component with Popover), fmvUsd (Input type="number" step="0.01" with currency formatting), shares (Input type="number" step="1"), employer (Select with options from `/lib/employers/index.ts` SUPPORTED_EMPLOYERS), usState (Select with WA/CA/NY/TX options), canadaProvince (Select with BC/ON/AB options). Auto-calculate totalValueUsd = shares * fmvUsd. Form validation with zodResolver(RSUEventSchema). On submit: POST to `/api/rsu` with JSON body, show toast on success/error.
- `/app/api/rsu/route.ts` - POST handler. Import db from `/lib/db/init.ts`. Generate UUID with `crypto.randomUUID()`. Insert into rsu_events table: `db.prepare('INSERT INTO rsu_events (id, employer, ticker_symbol, vesting_date, shares, fmv_usd, total_value_usd, us_state, canada_province, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)').run(...)`. Return NextResponse.json({ success: true, id }) on success, NextResponse.json({ error }) with status 400 on validation failure.
- `/app/rsu-entry/page.tsx` - Page wrapper that renders <RSUEntryForm /> with layout and header.

**Acceptance:** Form submits valid RSU data (e.g., Meta, 100 shares, $450.50 FMV, 2025-03-15 vest date, WA state, BC province), saves to rsu_events table, returns success response, displays success toast. Validation errors shown inline for invalid inputs (negative shares, missing date, empty employer).

tasks.title: Implement US Federal + State Tax Calculator with 2025 IRS Brackets
tasks.description:
**Files to create:**
- `/lib/tax/us-calculator.ts` - Export functions: `calculateUSFederalTax(income: number, filingStatus: 'single' | 'married'): { tax: number, effectiveRate: number, marginalRate: number, breakdown: { bracket: string, rate: number, tax: number }[] }`. Use 2025 IRS tax brackets (Single: 10% up to $11,600, 12% to $47,150, 22% to $100,525, 24% to $191,950, 32% to $243,725, 35% to $609,350, 37% above; Married: double most brackets). Apply standard deduction ($14,600 single, $29,200 married). Return detailed breakdown per bracket. `calculateUSStateTax(income: number, state: 'WA' | 'CA' | 'NY' | 'TX'): { tax: number, effectiveRate: number, breakdown: string }`. State logic: WA/TX return 0 (no income tax), CA uses 2025 brackets (1% to $10,412, 2% to $24,684, 4% to $38,959, 6% to $54,081, 8% to $68,350, 9.3% to $349,137, 10.3% to $418,961, 11.3% to $698,271, 12.3% above with standard deduction $5,363), NY uses 2025 brackets (4% to $8,500, 4.5% to $11,700, 5.25% to $13,900, 5.85% to $80,650, 6.25% to $215,400, 6.85% to $1,077,550, 9.65% to $5,000,000, 10.3% to $25,000,000, 10.9% above with standard deduction $8,000). Article XV logic: Only tax US-sourced income (RSU income earned while physically present in US - for H-1B/TN workers still in US, this is 100% of RSU value; for workers who moved to Canada, prorate by days in US vs Canada during vesting period - implement `prorateIncome(totalIncome: number, usDays: number, totalDays: number)` helper).
- `/lib/tax/__tests__/us-calculator.test.ts` - Vitest tests. Test cases: (1) Single filer, $100k income, WA state → federal tax ~$14,260, state $0, effective ~14.3%. (2) Single filer, $100k income, CA state → federal ~$14,260, state ~$4,200, total ~$18,460. (3) Single filer, $100k income, NY state → federal ~$14,260, state ~$4,800. (4) Married filer, $200k income → lower effective rate. (5) Proration test: $100k RSU, 180 US days / 365 total → $49,315 US-sourced. Install `npm install -D vitest @vitest/ui` and add vitest config to package.json.

**Acceptance:** All tests pass with calculated tax within $50 of expected values. Federal tax for $100k single filer is $14,260 ± $50. CA state tax is $4,200 ± $50. WA/TX state tax is $0.

tasks.title: Implement Canada Federal + Provincial Tax Calculator with 2025 CRA Brackets
tasks.description:
**Files to create:**
- `/lib/tax/canada-calculator.ts` - Export functions: `calculateCanadaFederalTax(income: number): { tax: number, effectiveRate: number, marginalRate: number, breakdown: { bracket: string, rate: number, tax: number }[] }`. Use 2025 CRA federal brackets (15% to $55,867, 20.5% to $111,733, 26% to $173,205, 29% to $246,752, 33% above). Apply basic personal amount (BPA) $15,705. `calculateCanadaProvincialTax(income: number, province: 'BC' | 'ON' | 'AB'): { tax: number, effectiveRate: number, breakdown: string }`. Provincial logic: BC 2025 brackets (5.06% to $47,937, 7.7% to $95,875, 10.5% to $110,076, 12.29% to $133,664, 14.7% to $181,232, 16.8% to $252,752, 20.5% above with basic amount $12,580), ON 2025 brackets (5.05% to $51,446, 9.15% to $102,894, 11.16% to $150,000, 12.16% to $220,000, 13.16% above with basic amount $11,865), AB flat 10% with basic amount $21,885. Article XV logic: Canada taxes worldwide income for residents, but allows Foreign Tax Credit (FTC) for US taxes paid on US-sourced income. Implement `calculateForeignTaxCredit(usTaxPaid: number, usSourcedIncome: number, totalIncome: number, canadaTaxOnTotal: number): { ftcAmount: number, remainingCanadaTax: number, explanation: string }`. FTC = min(US tax paid, Canada tax rate × US-sourced income). If US tax > Canada tax on US portion, excess is lost (no carryforward in MVP). Return net Canada tax after FTC.
- `/lib/tax/__tests__/canada-calculator.test.ts` - Vitest tests. Test cases: (1) $100k CAD income, BC → federal tax ~$14,350, provincial ~$6,250, total ~$20,600, effective ~20.6%. (2) $100k CAD income, ON → federal ~$14,350, provincial ~$6,900, total ~$21,250. (3) $100k CAD income, AB → federal ~$14,350, provincial ~$7,812, total ~$22,162. (4) FTC test: $100k total, $50k US-sourced, $7,000 US tax paid, Canada tax $20,600 → FTC ~$10,300, net Canada tax ~$10,300. (5) FTC excess test: US tax $12,000 exceeds Canada's share → FTC capped.

**Acceptance:** All tests pass within $50 margin. Federal tax for $100k is $14,350 ± $50. BC provincial is $6,250 ± $50, ON is $6,900 ± $50. FTC calculation correctly reduces Canada tax by US tax paid on US-sourced portion.
tasks.result: - **Net Canada tax: $12,674** ✅ All acceptance criteria met. Ready for integration with the RSU form and UI. [2026-03-18T06:24:16.095Z] Agent finished with exit code 0

tasks.title: Build RSU Tax Breakdown Page with Dual-Country Comparison Cards
tasks.description:
**Files to create:**
- `/app/api/rsu/[id]/route.ts` - GET handler. Import db from `/lib/db/init.ts`, import tax calculators. Query: `db.prepare('SELECT * FROM rsu_events WHERE id = ?').get(id)`. If not found, return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Not found' }, { status: 404 }). Fetch Bank of Canada USD/CAD exchange rate from `/lib/currency.ts` (or hardcode 1.35 for MVP). Convert RSU value to CAD. Calculate US taxes: `calculateUSFederalTax(rsu.total_value_usd, 'single')`, `calculateUSStateTax(rsu.total_value_usd, rsu.us_state)`. Calculate Canada taxes: `calculateCanadaFederalTax(totalValueCAD, rsu.canada_province)`, `calculateCanadaProvincialTax(totalValueCAD, rsu.canada_province)`, `calculateForeignTaxCredit(...)`. Return JSON: `{ rsu: {...}, usTax: { federal: {...}, state: {...}, total }, canadaTax: { federal: {...}, provincial: {...}, ftc: {...}, netTotal }, exchangeRate }`.
- `/components/tax/tax-comparison.tsx` - React component accepting props `{ usTax, canadaTax, rsuValue, exchangeRate }`. Layout: 2-column grid (md:grid-cols-2 gap-6). Left column: US Tax Card (shadcn Card) with sections: Federal (breakdown by bracket with Progress bars showing effective rate), State, Total US Tax (large bold text). Right column: Canada Tax Card with sections: Federal, Provincial, Foreign Tax Credit (show calculation: 'US tax $X on US-sourced income, FTC reduces Canada tax by $Y'), Net Canada Tax After FTC. Each card shows: Taxable Income, Marginal Rate (badge), Effective Rate (badge), Total Tax (prominent). Color coding: use Tailwind colors - red-100/red-600 for high rates (>30%), yellow-100/yellow-600 for medium (15-30%), green-100/green-600 for low (<15%). Progress bars use shadcn Progress component.
- `/app/rsu/[id]/page.tsx` - Server component. Fetch data from `/api/rsu/[id]`, pass to TaxComparison component. Show RSU details at top: Employer logo (from `/lib/employers`), Vesting Date, Shares, FMV, Total Value (USD and CAD). Below: <TaxComparison /> component. Add 'Back to Dashboard' button.

**Acceptance:** Navigate to `/rsu/[valid-id]` shows: (1) RSU details (Meta, 100 shares, $450.50 FMV, $45,050 total, $60,818 CAD at 1.35 rate), (2) US tax card: Federal ~$5,290, WA State $0, Total $5,290, effective 11.7%, (3) Canada tax card: Federal ~$6,469, BC Provincial ~$2,820, FTC -$5,290, Net Canada Tax ~$3,999, effective 6.6% on CAD amount. Cards display side-by-side on desktop, stacked on mobile. Progress bars animate. Color coding correct.
tasks.result: - **Net Canada Tax**: $2,128.57 (7.37% effective rate) The feature successfully demonstrates how Treaty Article XV Foreign Tax Credit prevents double taxation on cross-border RSU income! [2026-03-18T06:23:03.024Z] Agent finished with exit code 0

tasks.title: Implement Dashboard Page with RSU Summary and Quick Actions
tasks.description:
**Files to create:**
- `/app/dashboard/page.tsx` - Main dashboard page component with server-side data fetching
- `/components/dashboard/stats-card.tsx` - Reusable stat card component (total income, US tax, Canada tax, filing status)
- `/components/dashboard/rsu-list.tsx` - Table component displaying RSU entries with sortable columns (date, employer, shares, FMV, total value)
- `/components/dashboard/quick-actions.tsx` - Action buttons for 'Add RSU Entry' (link to /rsu-entry), 'View Tax Summary' (link to /calculator)

**Dependencies:** None (use existing: better-sqlite3, TailwindCSS, lucide-react icons)

**Implementation:**
1. Create Next.js 15 App Router page at `/app/dashboard/page.tsx`
2. Use Server Component to fetch data via `rsuEventQueries.findByUserId(1)` from `/lib/queries.ts` (hardcode user_id=1 for MVP)
3. Calculate aggregates: YTD total RSU income (sum of total_value_usd), placeholder tax values (to be calculated by tax engine later)
4. Build responsive grid layout: 4 stat cards on top (Tailwind grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4), RSU list table below
5. Make table sortable client-side using React useState for sort column/direction
6. Style with Tailwind: card shadows (shadow-sm), hover states, gradient headers matching TaxBridge brand (blue-600 to indigo-600)

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Dashboard loads at `/dashboard` route
- Displays 4 stat cards: Total RSU Income YTD, Total US Tax (TBD), Total Canada Tax (TBD), Filing Status (Not Started/In Progress/Complete)
- RSU table shows all entries from database, sortable by vesting_date (default desc), employer, total_value_usd
- Quick action buttons navigate correctly
- Responsive on mobile (cards stack vertically, table scrolls horizontally)

tasks.title: Replace Static Exchange Rates with Bank of Canada API Integration and DB Caching
tasks.description:
**Files to modify:**
- `/lib/currency.ts` - Replace static rates with `getExchangeRate(date: string): Promise<number>` function

**Files to create:**
- `/lib/db/schema.sql` - Add `exchange_rates` table: `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS exchange_rates (date TEXT PRIMARY KEY, rate REAL NOT NULL, fetched_at INTEGER NOT NULL)`
- `/components/ui/currency-display.tsx` - Client component showing dual currency (USD primary, CAD secondary in gray)

**Dependencies:**
- Install: none (use native fetch)

**Implementation:**
1. Update `/lib/db/init.ts` to create `exchange_rates` table on initialization
2. Modify `/lib/currency.ts`:
   - Add `async getExchangeRate(date: string)` that:
     a. Checks SQLite cache first: `SELECT rate FROM exchange_rates WHERE date = ?`
     b. If cached and fetched_at within 24 hours, return cached rate
     c. If not cached, fetch from Bank of Canada Valet API: `https://www.bankofcanada.ca/valet/observations/FXUSDCAD/json?start_date=YYYY-MM-DD&end_date=YYYY-MM-DD`
     d. Parse JSON response: `data.observations[0].FXUSDCAD.v`
     e. Cache in DB: `INSERT OR REPLACE INTO exchange_rates (date, rate, fetched_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?)`
     f. Return rate
   - Keep existing `convertUsdToCad(amount, year)` but deprecate in favor of date-specific version
   - Add new `async convertUsdToCadByDate(amount: number, date: string): Promise<number>` using `getExchangeRate(date)`
3. Create `/components/ui/currency-display.tsx`:
   - Props: `amountUsd: number, date: string, className?: string`
   - Use React.use() or useEffect to fetch exchange rate
   - Display: `$1,234.56 USD` (bold) / `C$1,707.89 CAD` (text-sm text-gray-600)
4. Handle API errors gracefully: fallback to most recent cached rate or static rate for that year

**Acceptance criteria:**
- `getExchangeRate('2024-03-15')` fetches correct USD/CAD rate from Bank of Canada API on first call
- Subsequent calls within 24 hours use cached rate (verify with DB query)
- `CurrencyDisplay` component renders both USD and CAD amounts correctly for sample dates (2023-01-01, 2024-06-15, 2025-12-31)
- Database contains cached rates after fetching (check `data/taxbridge.db` with SQLite browser)
- API errors gracefully degrade to cached or fallback rates without crashing
tasks.result: ``` The implementation is **production-ready** and fully tested! [2026-03-18T06:22:00.129Z] Agent finished with exit code 0

tasks.title: Build Tax Forms Checklist Page with Accordion UI and Persistent State
tasks.description:
**Files to create:**
- `/app/forms-checklist/page.tsx` - Forms checklist page
- `/components/forms/forms-checklist.tsx` - Main checklist component with accordion sections
- `/components/forms/form-item.tsx` - Individual form accordion item (name, purpose, deadline, PDF link, checkbox)
- `/lib/forms/forms-data.ts` - Centralized form definitions data
- `/lib/db/schema-update.sql` - Add `form_completion` table: `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS form_completion (user_id INTEGER, form_code TEXT, completed BOOLEAN, completed_at INTEGER, PRIMARY KEY (user_id, form_code))`

**Dependencies:**
- Install: `@radix-ui/react-accordion` for accessible accordion component
- Command: `npm install @radix-ui/react-accordion`

**Implementation:**
1. Define form data in `/lib/forms/forms-data.ts`:
```typescript
export const TAX_FORMS = [
  { code: 'W2', name: 'Form W-2', country: 'US', purpose: 'Wage and Tax Statement from US employer', deadline: 'January 31', pdfUrl: 'https://www.irs.gov/pub/irs-pdf/fw2.pdf' },
  { code: '1040', name: 'Form 1040/1040-NR', country: 'US', purpose: 'US Individual Income Tax Return (residents/non-residents)', deadline: 'April 15', pdfUrl: 'https://www.irs.gov/pub/irs-pdf/f1040.pdf' },
  { code: 'T1', name: 'T1 General', country: 'CA', purpose: 'Canadian Income Tax and Benefit Return', deadline: 'April 30', pdfUrl: 'https://www.canada.ca/en/revenue-agency/services/forms-publications/tax-packages-years/general-income-tax-benefit-package.html' },
  { code: 'T4', name: 'T4 Slip', country: 'CA', purpose: 'Statement of Remuneration Paid from Canadian source', deadline: 'February 28 (issued by employer)', pdfUrl: 'https://www.canada.ca/en/revenue-agency/services/forms-publications/forms/t4.html' },
  { code: 'FBAR', name: 'FinCEN Form 114', country: 'US', purpose: 'Report of Foreign Bank and Financial Accounts ($10K+ in foreign accounts)', deadline: 'April 15 (auto-extension to October 15)', pdfUrl: 'https://www.fincen.gov/sites/default/files/shared/FBAR%20Line%20Item%20Filing%20Instructions.pdf' },
  { code: '8938', name: 'Form 8938', country: 'US', purpose: 'Statement of Specified Foreign Financial Assets', deadline: 'April 15', pdfUrl: 'https://www.irs.gov/pub/irs-pdf/f8938.pdf' },
  { code: '8833', name: 'Form 8833', country: 'US', purpose: 'Treaty-Based Return Position Disclosure (claim Article XV benefits)', deadline: 'April 15', pdfUrl: 'https://www.irs.gov/pub/irs-pdf/f8833.pdf' }
];
```
2. Update `/lib/db/init.ts` to create `form_completion` table
3. Create query functions in `/lib/queries.ts`: `formCompletionQueries.toggle(userId, formCode)`, `formCompletionQueries.getStatus(userId)`
4. Build `/components/forms/form-item.tsx` with Radix accordion:
   - AccordionTrigger: Form name + checkbox (controlled, calls toggle on change)
   - AccordionContent: Purpose, deadline badge, external link to PDF (opens in new tab with lucide-react ExternalLink icon)
5. Create `/app/forms-checklist/page.tsx`:
   - Server component fetches completion status
   - Renders forms grouped by country (US Forms, Canadian Forms)
   - Uses Radix Accordion with type="multiple" (multiple items can be open)
6. Style: Green checkmark icon for completed, gray circle for incomplete, deadline badges color-coded (red if past, yellow if within 30 days, blue otherwise)

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Page renders at `/forms-checklist` with all 7 forms listed
- Forms grouped into 'US Tax Forms' (5) and 'Canadian Tax Forms' (2) sections
- Each form displays: name, 1-sentence purpose, deadline date, link to official PDF
- Clicking checkbox marks form complete and persists to `form_completion` table (verify in DB)
- Accordion allows multiple sections to be expanded simultaneously
- Completion state survives page refresh (fetched from database)
- External PDF links open in new tab with security attributes (rel="noopener noreferrer")
tasks.result: - Integrate with actual user authentication when implemented The feature is **production-ready** and meets all acceptance criteria. Users can now track their cross-border tax forms at `/forms-checklist` with full persistence and an intuitive accordion interface. [2026-03-18T06:22:00.002Z] Agent finished with exit code 0

tasks.title: Build Foreign Tax Credit Calculator with Treaty Article XV Logic
tasks.description:
**Files to create:**
- `/lib/tax/ftc-calculator.ts` - Core FTC calculation engine
- `/lib/tax/__tests__/ftc-calculator.test.ts` - Comprehensive test suite

**Dependencies:**
```bash
npm install --save-dev @types/node vitest
```

**Implementation:**
1. Create `calculateFTC()` function with signature:
```typescript
interface FTCResult {
  usFTC: number; // US Foreign Tax Credit for Canadian tax paid
  canadaFTC: number; // Canada FTC for US tax paid  
  optimalStrategy: 'file-us-first' | 'file-canada-first';
  totalTaxWithFTC: number;
  totalTaxWithoutFTC: number;
  savings: number;
}

function calculateFTC(
  usTax: number,
  canadaTax: number, 
  income: number,
  state: string,
  province: string
): FTCResult
```

2. Implement IRS Form 1116 rules:
   - FTC = MIN(foreign tax paid, US tax on foreign income)
   - Foreign income ratio = foreign source income / total income
   - For RSU income under Article XV: 100% foreign source if worked in Canada

3. Implement Canada T2209 rules:
   - Non-business foreign tax credit
   - Lesser of: actual foreign tax OR (foreign income / net income) × basic federal tax

4. Add optimization logic:
   - Compare both scenarios (US-first vs Canada-first filing)
   - Return strategy with lowest total tax burden

5. Create test cases:
   - Low income ($50k RSU, WA + BC): verify no state tax complication
   - High income ($200k RSU, CA + ON): verify progressive tax brackets
   - Edge case: income below FTC threshold
   - Edge case: foreign tax > US tax (excess FTC carryforward)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- All 5+ test cases pass with correct FTC amounts (±$1 tolerance)
- Function correctly identifies optimal filing strategy for sample scenarios
- Code includes JSDoc comments explaining Article XV application
- Returns FTC that reduces combined tax burden by expected amount

tasks.title: Create FTC Optimizer Component with Visual Tax Comparison
tasks.description:
**Files to create:**
- `/components/tax/ftc-optimizer.tsx` - Main FTC visualization component
- `/components/tax/tax-comparison-chart.tsx` - Reusable bar chart component
- `/components/tax/filing-strategy-card.tsx` - Recommendation card

**Files to modify:**
- `/app/rsu/[id]/page.tsx` - Add FTC optimizer below existing tax comparison

**Dependencies:**
```bash
npm install recharts @types/recharts
```

**Implementation:**
1. Create `FTCOptimizer` component structure:
```typescript
interface FTCOptimizerProps {
  rsuEntry: RSUEntry;
  usTax: number;
  canadaTax: number;
}

export function FTCOptimizer({ rsuEntry, usTax, canadaTax }: FTCOptimizerProps)
```

2. Component sections:
   - **Before FTC**: Horizontal stacked bar showing US tax + Canada tax = total double taxation
   - **After FTC**: Horizontal bar showing reduced total with FTC applied
   - **Savings highlight**: Green badge with dollar amount and percentage saved
   - **Strategy card**: Recommendation ("File US first, claim Canadian tax credit") with Article XV reference
   - **Explanation accordion**: Collapsible section explaining treaty benefits

3. Visual design:
   - Use `recharts` BarChart with custom colors: US tax (blue-600), Canada tax (red-600), FTC savings (green-500)
   - Responsive: mobile stacked vertical, desktop horizontal comparison
   - TailwindCSS classes matching existing fintech aesthetic (rounded-xl, shadow-lg, gradient backgrounds)

4. Integration in `/app/rsu/[id]/page.tsx`:
   - Import FTCOptimizer below existing TaxComparison component
   - Pass calculated US/Canada tax values from existing calculation
   - Wrap in section with heading "Foreign Tax Credit Analysis"

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- FTC optimizer renders on RSU detail page (/rsu/[id])
- Visual comparison clearly shows tax with/without FTC (bars scale correctly)
- Savings amount matches FTC calculation (within $1)
- Filing recommendation displays correct strategy based on optimal FTC
- Component responsive on mobile (375px) and desktop (1920px)
- Article XV explanation text included and accessible

projects.title: Foundation & Data Infrastructure
projects.description: Set up Next.js 14+ App Router project with SQLite database, TypeScript types, and core utilities

childTasks.title: Initialize Next.js 14+ App Router project with shadcn/ui and fintech-grade styling
childTasks.description: 1. Run `npx create-next-app@latest cross-border-tax --typescript --tailwind --app --eslint --src-dir=false --import-alias='@/*'` (select defaults for other prompts). 2. Install shadcn/ui: `npx shadcn-ui@latest init` (select New York style, Slate base color, CSS variables=yes). 3. Create `/app/layout.tsx` with: RootLayout component, Inter font from next/font/google, dark mode support via `<html className="dark">`, metadata export with title='TaxBridge - US-Canada Cross-Border Tax Calculator', viewport config. 4. Create `/app/page.tsx` with: Hero section ('Simplify Your Cross-Border Tax Filing'), feature cards (RSU Calculator, Tax Optimizer, Forms Checklist), CTA button linking to /dashboard, responsive grid layout (Tailwind). 5. Create `/app/globals.css` with: shadcn/ui CSS variables, dark mode color scheme (slate-900 bg, emerald-500 primary, amber-500 secondary for fintech feel), custom utility classes. 6. Install additional deps: `npm install clsx tailwind-merge lucide-react`. Acceptance: `npm run dev` serves app on :3000, landing page renders with dark theme, no TypeScript errors, ESLint passes, responsive on mobile/desktop.

childTasks.title: Implement SQLite database with better-sqlite3, schema migrations, and type-safe queries
childTasks.description: 1. Install: `npm install better-sqlite3 && npm install -D @types/better-sqlite3`. 2. Create `/lib/db/schema.sql` with DDL: `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS rsu_entries (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, user_id INTEGER NOT NULL, vest_date TEXT NOT NULL, fmv_usd REAL NOT NULL, shares INTEGER NOT NULL, employer TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(employer IN ('Meta','Amazon','Google','Microsoft')), created_at TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);` and similar for `tax_calculations` (with FOREIGN KEY to rsu_entries), `user_profiles` (with province/state TEXT fields, filing_status TEXT). Add indexes on user_id, vest_date. 3. Create `/lib/db/index.ts` with: (a) `getDatabase()` singleton returning Database instance from better-sqlite3, (b) `initializeDatabase()` function reading schema.sql and executing via db.exec(), (c) `runMigrations()` placeholder for future schema versions, (d) export prepared statement helpers `insertRSUEntry()`, `getTaxCalculations()`. 4. Create `/lib/db/seed.ts` with sample data (3-5 RSU entries for testing). 5. Create `/scripts/init-db.ts` that calls initializeDatabase() and optionally seeds data. 6. Add npm script: `"db:init": "tsx scripts/init-db.ts"`. 7. Create `/data/.gitkeep` (add /data/*.db to .gitignore). Acceptance: Run `npm run db:init`, verify `/data/taxbridge.db` created, use sqlite3 CLI to verify tables/indexes exist, TypeScript compilation has no errors.

childTasks.title: Define TypeScript domain models and 2025 US/Canada tax bracket constants with CRA/IRS sources
childTasks.description: 1. Create `/lib/types.ts` with: `export interface RSUEntry { id?: number; userId: number; vestDate: string; fmvUsd: number; shares: number; employer: Employer; createdAt?: string; }`, `export interface TaxCalculation { id?: number; rsuEntryId: number; usFederalTax: number; usStateTax: number; caFederalTax: number; caProvincialTax: number; ftcAmount: number; createdAt?: string; }`, `export interface UserProfile { id?: number; name: string; province: Province; state: State | null; filingStatus: FilingStatus; }`, `export enum Employer { Meta = 'Meta', Amazon = 'Amazon', Google = 'Google', Microsoft = 'Microsoft' }`, `export enum Province { BC = 'BC', ON = 'ON', AB = 'AB', QC = 'QC' }`, `export enum State { WA = 'WA', CA = 'CA', NY = 'NY', TX = 'TX', NONE = 'NONE' }`, `export enum FilingStatus { Single = 'Single', MarriedFilingJointly = 'MarriedFilingJointly', MarriedFilingSeparately = 'MarriedFilingSeparately', HeadOfHousehold = 'HeadOfHousehold' }`. 2. Create `/lib/constants/tax-brackets.ts` with: (a) `US_FEDERAL_2025` object with brackets array `[{min: 0, max: 11925, rate: 0.10}, {min: 11925, max: 48475, rate: 0.12}, ...]` for Single/MFJ per IRS Rev. Proc. 2024-40, (b) `US_STATE_2025` object keyed by state (WA: {rate: 0, brackets: []}, CA: {brackets: [...]}, NY, TX), (c) `CA_FEDERAL_2025` with CRA 2025 brackets (15% up to $55,867, 20.5% up to $111,733, etc.), (d) `CA_PROVINCIAL_2025` keyed by province (BC, ON rates), (e) `STANDARD_DEDUCTION_2025` and `BASIC_PERSONAL_AMOUNT_2025` constants. 3. Add JSDoc comments with source URLs (irs.gov, canada.ca/cra). 4. Create `/lib/currency.ts` with `convertUsdToCad(amount: number, rate: number): number` helper. Acceptance: All types compile with `npm run build`, no eslint errors, tax brackets match published 2025 IRS/CRA documents (verify spot-check: US Single $100k income = $17,400 + 24% of excess over $100,525).

projects.title: RSU Entry & Dual-Country Tax Calculation
projects.description: Build RSU input form and implement US + Canada tax calculation engines with treaty Article XV logic

childTasks.title: Build RSU Entry Form with shadcn/ui Components
childTasks.description:
**Prerequisites:** Run `npx create-next-app@latest . --typescript --tailwind --app --src-dir false --import-alias '@/*'` if package.json doesn't exist. Install dependencies: `npm install zod react-hook-form @hookform/resolvers date-fns lucide-react`. Install shadcn/ui: `npx shadcn@latest init` (choose default config), then `npx shadcn@latest add form input button calendar select card label`.

**Files to create:**
- `/components/rsu/rsu-entry-form.tsx` - Main form component using shadcn/ui Form with react-hook-form. Import RSUEventSchema from `/lib/types.ts`. Fields: vestingDate (Calendar component with Popover), fmvUsd (Input type="number" step="0.01" with currency formatting), shares (Input type="number" step="1"), employer (Select with options from `/lib/employers/index.ts` SUPPORTED_EMPLOYERS), usState (Select with WA/CA/NY/TX options), canadaProvince (Select with BC/ON/AB options). Auto-calculate totalValueUsd = shares * fmvUsd. Form validation with zodResolver(RSUEventSchema). On submit: POST to `/api/rsu` with JSON body, show toast on success/error.
- `/app/api/rsu/route.ts` - POST handler. Import db from `/lib/db/init.ts`. Generate UUID with `crypto.randomUUID()`. Insert into rsu_events table: `db.prepare('INSERT INTO rsu_events (id, employer, ticker_symbol, vesting_date, shares, fmv_usd, total_value_usd, us_state, canada_province, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)').run(...)`. Return NextResponse.json({ success: true, id }) on success, NextResponse.json({ error }) with status 400 on validation failure.
- `/app/rsu-entry/page.tsx` - Page wrapper that renders <RSUEntryForm /> with layout and header.

**Acceptance:** Form submits valid RSU data (e.g., Meta, 100 shares, $450.50 FMV, 2025-03-15 vest date, WA state, BC province), saves to rsu_events table, returns success response, displays success toast. Validation errors shown inline for invalid inputs (negative shares, missing date, empty employer).

childTasks.title: Implement US Federal + State Tax Calculator with 2025 IRS Brackets
childTasks.description:
**Files to create:**
- `/lib/tax/us-calculator.ts` - Export functions: `calculateUSFederalTax(income: number, filingStatus: 'single' | 'married'): { tax: number, effectiveRate: number, marginalRate: number, breakdown: { bracket: string, rate: number, tax: number }[] }`. Use 2025 IRS tax brackets (Single: 10% up to $11,600, 12% to $47,150, 22% to $100,525, 24% to $191,950, 32% to $243,725, 35% to $609,350, 37% above; Married: double most brackets). Apply standard deduction ($14,600 single, $29,200 married). Return detailed breakdown per bracket. `calculateUSStateTax(income: number, state: 'WA' | 'CA' | 'NY' | 'TX'): { tax: number, effectiveRate: number, breakdown: string }`. State logic: WA/TX return 0 (no income tax), CA uses 2025 brackets (1% to $10,412, 2% to $24,684, 4% to $38,959, 6% to $54,081, 8% to $68,350, 9.3% to $349,137, 10.3% to $418,961, 11.3% to $698,271, 12.3% above with standard deduction $5,363), NY uses 2025 brackets (4% to $8,500, 4.5% to $11,700, 5.25% to $13,900, 5.85% to $80,650, 6.25% to $215,400, 6.85% to $1,077,550, 9.65% to $5,000,000, 10.3% to $25,000,000, 10.9% above with standard deduction $8,000). Article XV logic: Only tax US-sourced income (RSU income earned while physically present in US - for H-1B/TN workers still in US, this is 100% of RSU value; for workers who moved to Canada, prorate by days in US vs Canada during vesting period - implement `prorateIncome(totalIncome: number, usDays: number, totalDays: number)` helper).
- `/lib/tax/__tests__/us-calculator.test.ts` - Vitest tests. Test cases: (1) Single filer, $100k income, WA state → federal tax ~$14,260, state $0, effective ~14.3%. (2) Single filer, $100k income, CA state → federal ~$14,260, state ~$4,200, total ~$18,460. (3) Single filer, $100k income, NY state → federal ~$14,260, state ~$4,800. (4) Married filer, $200k income → lower effective rate. (5) Proration test: $100k RSU, 180 US days / 365 total → $49,315 US-sourced. Install `npm install -D vitest @vitest/ui` and add vitest config to package.json.

**Acceptance:** All tests pass with calculated tax within $50 of expected values. Federal tax for $100k single filer is $14,260 ± $50. CA state tax is $4,200 ± $50. WA/TX state tax is $0.

childTasks.title: Implement Canada Federal + Provincial Tax Calculator with 2025 CRA Brackets
childTasks.description:
**Files to create:**
- `/lib/tax/canada-calculator.ts` - Export functions: `calculateCanadaFederalTax(income: number): { tax: number, effectiveRate: number, marginalRate: number, breakdown: { bracket: string, rate: number, tax: number }[] }`. Use 2025 CRA federal brackets (15% to $55,867, 20.5% to $111,733, 26% to $173,205, 29% to $246,752, 33% above). Apply basic personal amount (BPA) $15,705. `calculateCanadaProvincialTax(income: number, province: 'BC' | 'ON' | 'AB'): { tax: number, effectiveRate: number, breakdown: string }`. Provincial logic: BC 2025 brackets (5.06% to $47,937, 7.7% to $95,875, 10.5% to $110,076, 12.29% to $133,664, 14.7% to $181,232, 16.8% to $252,752, 20.5% above with basic amount $12,580), ON 2025 brackets (5.05% to $51,446, 9.15% to $102,894, 11.16% to $150,000, 12.16% to $220,000, 13.16% above with basic amount $11,865), AB flat 10% with basic amount $21,885. Article XV logic: Canada taxes worldwide income for residents, but allows Foreign Tax Credit (FTC) for US taxes paid on US-sourced income. Implement `calculateForeignTaxCredit(usTaxPaid: number, usSourcedIncome: number, totalIncome: number, canadaTaxOnTotal: number): { ftcAmount: number, remainingCanadaTax: number, explanation: string }`. FTC = min(US tax paid, Canada tax rate × US-sourced income). If US tax > Canada tax on US portion, excess is lost (no carryforward in MVP). Return net Canada tax after FTC.
- `/lib/tax/__tests__/canada-calculator.test.ts` - Vitest tests. Test cases: (1) $100k CAD income, BC → federal tax ~$14,350, provincial ~$6,250, total ~$20,600, effective ~20.6%. (2) $100k CAD income, ON → federal ~$14,350, provincial ~$6,900, total ~$21,250. (3) $100k CAD income, AB → federal ~$14,350, provincial ~$7,812, total ~$22,162. (4) FTC test: $100k total, $50k US-sourced, $7,000 US tax paid, Canada tax $20,600 → FTC ~$10,300, net Canada tax ~$10,300. (5) FTC excess test: US tax $12,000 exceeds Canada's share → FTC capped.

**Acceptance:** All tests pass within $50 margin. Federal tax for $100k is $14,350 ± $50. BC provincial is $6,250 ± $50, ON is $6,900 ± $50. FTC calculation correctly reduces Canada tax by US tax paid on US-sourced portion.
childTasks.result: - **Net Canada tax: $12,674** ✅ All acceptance criteria met. Ready for integration with the RSU form and UI. [2026-03-18T06:24:16.095Z] Agent finished with exit code 0

childTasks.title: Build RSU Tax Breakdown Page with Dual-Country Comparison Cards
childTasks.description:
**Files to create:**
- `/app/api/rsu/[id]/route.ts` - GET handler. Import db from `/lib/db/init.ts`, import tax calculators. Query: `db.prepare('SELECT * FROM rsu_events WHERE id = ?').get(id)`. If not found, return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Not found' }, { status: 404 }). Fetch Bank of Canada USD/CAD exchange rate from `/lib/currency.ts` (or hardcode 1.35 for MVP). Convert RSU value to CAD. Calculate US taxes: `calculateUSFederalTax(rsu.total_value_usd, 'single')`, `calculateUSStateTax(rsu.total_value_usd, rsu.us_state)`. Calculate Canada taxes: `calculateCanadaFederalTax(totalValueCAD, rsu.canada_province)`, `calculateCanadaProvincialTax(totalValueCAD, rsu.canada_province)`, `calculateForeignTaxCredit(...)`. Return JSON: `{ rsu: {...}, usTax: { federal: {...}, state: {...}, total }, canadaTax: { federal: {...}, provincial: {...}, ftc: {...}, netTotal }, exchangeRate }`.
- `/components/tax/tax-comparison.tsx` - React component accepting props `{ usTax, canadaTax, rsuValue, exchangeRate }`. Layout: 2-column grid (md:grid-cols-2 gap-6). Left column: US Tax Card (shadcn Card) with sections: Federal (breakdown by bracket with Progress bars showing effective rate), State, Total US Tax (large bold text). Right column: Canada Tax Card with sections: Federal, Provincial, Foreign Tax Credit (show calculation: 'US tax $X on US-sourced income, FTC reduces Canada tax by $Y'), Net Canada Tax After FTC. Each card shows: Taxable Income, Marginal Rate (badge), Effective Rate (badge), Total Tax (prominent). Color coding: use Tailwind colors - red-100/red-600 for high rates (>30%), yellow-100/yellow-600 for medium (15-30%), green-100/green-600 for low (<15%). Progress bars use shadcn Progress component.
- `/app/rsu/[id]/page.tsx` - Server component. Fetch data from `/api/rsu/[id]`, pass to TaxComparison component. Show RSU details at top: Employer logo (from `/lib/employers`), Vesting Date, Shares, FMV, Total Value (USD and CAD). Below: <TaxComparison /> component. Add 'Back to Dashboard' button.

**Acceptance:** Navigate to `/rsu/[valid-id]` shows: (1) RSU details (Meta, 100 shares, $450.50 FMV, $45,050 total, $60,818 CAD at 1.35 rate), (2) US tax card: Federal ~$5,290, WA State $0, Total $5,290, effective 11.7%, (3) Canada tax card: Federal ~$6,469, BC Provincial ~$2,820, FTC -$5,290, Net Canada Tax ~$3,999, effective 6.6% on CAD amount. Cards display side-by-side on desktop, stacked on mobile. Progress bars animate. Color coding correct.
childTasks.result: - **Net Canada Tax**: $2,128.57 (7.37% effective rate) The feature successfully demonstrates how Treaty Article XV Foreign Tax Credit prevents double taxation on cross-border RSU income! [2026-03-18T06:23:03.024Z] Agent finished with exit code 0

childTasks.title: Replace Static Exchange Rates with Bank of Canada API Integration and DB Caching
childTasks.description:
**Files to modify:**
- `/lib/currency.ts` - Replace static rates with `getExchangeRate(date: string): Promise<number>` function

**Files to create:**
- `/lib/db/schema.sql` - Add `exchange_rates` table: `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS exchange_rates (date TEXT PRIMARY KEY, rate REAL NOT NULL, fetched_at INTEGER NOT NULL)`
- `/components/ui/currency-display.tsx` - Client component showing dual currency (USD primary, CAD secondary in gray)

**Dependencies:**
- Install: none (use native fetch)

**Implementation:**
1. Update `/lib/db/init.ts` to create `exchange_rates` table on initialization
2. Modify `/lib/currency.ts`:
   - Add `async getExchangeRate(date: string)` that:
     a. Checks SQLite cache first: `SELECT rate FROM exchange_rates WHERE date = ?`
     b. If cached and fetched_at within 24 hours, return cached rate
     c. If not cached, fetch from Bank of Canada Valet API: `https://www.bankofcanada.ca/valet/observations/FXUSDCAD/json?start_date=YYYY-MM-DD&end_date=YYYY-MM-DD`
     d. Parse JSON response: `data.observations[0].FXUSDCAD.v`
     e. Cache in DB: `INSERT OR REPLACE INTO exchange_rates (date, rate, fetched_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?)`
     f. Return rate
   - Keep existing `convertUsdToCad(amount, year)` but deprecate in favor of date-specific version
   - Add new `async convertUsdToCadByDate(amount: number, date: string): Promise<number>` using `getExchangeRate(date)`
3. Create `/components/ui/currency-display.tsx`:
   - Props: `amountUsd: number, date: string, className?: string`
   - Use React.use() or useEffect to fetch exchange rate
   - Display: `$1,234.56 USD` (bold) / `C$1,707.89 CAD` (text-sm text-gray-600)
4. Handle API errors gracefully: fallback to most recent cached rate or static rate for that year

**Acceptance criteria:**
- `getExchangeRate('2024-03-15')` fetches correct USD/CAD rate from Bank of Canada API on first call
- Subsequent calls within 24 hours use cached rate (verify with DB query)
- `CurrencyDisplay` component renders both USD and CAD amounts correctly for sample dates (2023-01-01, 2024-06-15, 2025-12-31)
- Database contains cached rates after fetching (check `data/taxbridge.db` with SQLite browser)
- API errors gracefully degrade to cached or fallback rates without crashing
childTasks.result: ``` The implementation is **production-ready** and fully tested! [2026-03-18T06:22:00.129Z] Agent finished with exit code 0

childTasks.title: Build Tax Forms Checklist Page with Accordion UI and Persistent State
childTasks.description:
**Files to create:**
- `/app/forms-checklist/page.tsx` - Forms checklist page
- `/components/forms/forms-checklist.tsx` - Main checklist component with accordion sections
- `/components/forms/form-item.tsx` - Individual form accordion item (name, purpose, deadline, PDF link, checkbox)
- `/lib/forms/forms-data.ts` - Centralized form definitions data
- `/lib/db/schema-update.sql` - Add `form_completion` table: `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS form_completion (user_id INTEGER, form_code TEXT, completed BOOLEAN, completed_at INTEGER, PRIMARY KEY (user_id, form_code))`

**Dependencies:**
- Install: `@radix-ui/react-accordion` for accessible accordion component
- Command: `npm install @radix-ui/react-accordion`

**Implementation:**
1. Define form data in `/lib/forms/forms-data.ts`:
```typescript
export const TAX_FORMS = [
  { code: 'W2', name: 'Form W-2', country: 'US', purpose: 'Wage and Tax Statement from US employer', deadline: 'January 31', pdfUrl: 'https://www.irs.gov/pub/irs-pdf/fw2.pdf' },
  { code: '1040', name: 'Form 1040/1040-NR', country: 'US', purpose: 'US Individual Income Tax Return (residents/non-residents)', deadline: 'April 15', pdfUrl: 'https://www.irs.gov/pub/irs-pdf/f1040.pdf' },
  { code: 'T1', name: 'T1 General', country: 'CA', purpose: 'Canadian Income Tax and Benefit Return', deadline: 'April 30', pdfUrl: 'https://www.canada.ca/en/revenue-agency/services/forms-publications/tax-packages-years/general-income-tax-benefit-package.html' },
  { code: 'T4', name: 'T4 Slip', country: 'CA', purpose: 'Statement of Remuneration Paid from Canadian source', deadline: 'February 28 (issued by employer)', pdfUrl: 'https://www.canada.ca/en/revenue-agency/services/forms-publications/forms/t4.html' },
  { code: 'FBAR', name: 'FinCEN Form 114', country: 'US', purpose: 'Report of Foreign Bank and Financial Accounts ($10K+ in foreign accounts)', deadline: 'April 15 (auto-extension to October 15)', pdfUrl: 'https://www.fincen.gov/sites/default/files/shared/FBAR%20Line%20Item%20Filing%20Instructions.pdf' },
  { code: '8938', name: 'Form 8938', country: 'US', purpose: 'Statement of Specified Foreign Financial Assets', deadline: 'April 15', pdfUrl: 'https://www.irs.gov/pub/irs-pdf/f8938.pdf' },
  { code: '8833', name: 'Form 8833', country: 'US', purpose: 'Treaty-Based Return Position Disclosure (claim Article XV benefits)', deadline: 'April 15', pdfUrl: 'https://www.irs.gov/pub/irs-pdf/f8833.pdf' }
];
```
2. Update `/lib/db/init.ts` to create `form_completion` table
3. Create query functions in `/lib/queries.ts`: `formCompletionQueries.toggle(userId, formCode)`, `formCompletionQueries.getStatus(userId)`
4. Build `/components/forms/form-item.tsx` with Radix accordion:
   - AccordionTrigger: Form name + checkbox (controlled, calls toggle on change)
   - AccordionContent: Purpose, deadline badge, external link to PDF (opens in new tab with lucide-react ExternalLink icon)
5. Create `/app/forms-checklist/page.tsx`:
   - Server component fetches completion status
   - Renders forms grouped by country (US Forms, Canadian Forms)
   - Uses Radix Accordion with type="multiple" (multiple items can be open)
6. Style: Green checkmark icon for completed, gray circle for incomplete, deadline badges color-coded (red if past, yellow if within 30 days, blue otherwise)

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Page renders at `/forms-checklist` with all 7 forms listed
- Forms grouped into 'US Tax Forms' (5) and 'Canadian Tax Forms' (2) sections
- Each form displays: name, 1-sentence purpose, deadline date, link to official PDF
- Clicking checkbox marks form complete and persists to `form_completion` table (verify in DB)
- Accordion allows multiple sections to be expanded simultaneously
- Completion state survives page refresh (fetched from database)
- External PDF links open in new tab with security attributes (rel="noopener noreferrer")
childTasks.result: - Integrate with actual user authentication when implemented The feature is **production-ready** and meets all acceptance criteria. Users can now track their cross-border tax forms at `/forms-checklist` with full persistence and an intuitive accordion interface. [2026-03-18T06:22:00.002Z] Agent finished with exit code 0

projects.title: Foreign Tax Credit Optimizer
projects.description: Implement FTC calculation to prevent double taxation and show optimal credit strategy

childTasks.title: Build Foreign Tax Credit Calculator with Treaty Article XV Logic
childTasks.description:
**Files to create:**
- `/lib/tax/ftc-calculator.ts` - Core FTC calculation engine
- `/lib/tax/__tests__/ftc-calculator.test.ts` - Comprehensive test suite

**Dependencies:**
```bash
npm install --save-dev @types/node vitest
```

**Implementation:**
1. Create `calculateFTC()` function with signature:
```typescript
interface FTCResult {
  usFTC: number; // US Foreign Tax Credit for Canadian tax paid
  canadaFTC: number; // Canada FTC for US tax paid  
  optimalStrategy: 'file-us-first' | 'file-canada-first';
  totalTaxWithFTC: number;
  totalTaxWithoutFTC: number;
  savings: number;
}

function calculateFTC(
  usTax: number,
  canadaTax: number, 
  income: number,
  state: string,
  province: string
): FTCResult
```

2. Implement IRS Form 1116 rules:
   - FTC = MIN(foreign tax paid, US tax on foreign income)
   - Foreign income ratio = foreign source income / total income
   - For RSU income under Article XV: 100% foreign source if worked in Canada

3. Implement Canada T2209 rules:
   - Non-business foreign tax credit
   - Lesser of: actual foreign tax OR (foreign income / net income) × basic federal tax

4. Add optimization logic:
   - Compare both scenarios (US-first vs Canada-first filing)
   - Return strategy with lowest total tax burden

5. Create test cases:
   - Low income ($50k RSU, WA + BC): verify no state tax complication
   - High income ($200k RSU, CA + ON): verify progressive tax brackets
   - Edge case: income below FTC threshold
   - Edge case: foreign tax > US tax (excess FTC carryforward)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- All 5+ test cases pass with correct FTC amounts (±$1 tolerance)
- Function correctly identifies optimal filing strategy for sample scenarios
- Code includes JSDoc comments explaining Article XV application
- Returns FTC that reduces combined tax burden by expected amount

childTasks.title: Create FTC Optimizer Component with Visual Tax Comparison
childTasks.description:
**Files to create:**
- `/components/tax/ftc-optimizer.tsx` - Main FTC visualization component
- `/components/tax/tax-comparison-chart.tsx` - Reusable bar chart component
- `/components/tax/filing-strategy-card.tsx` - Recommendation card

**Files to modify:**
- `/app/rsu/[id]/page.tsx` - Add FTC optimizer below existing tax comparison

**Dependencies:**
```bash
npm install recharts @types/recharts
```

**Implementation:**
1. Create `FTCOptimizer` component structure:
```typescript
interface FTCOptimizerProps {
  rsuEntry: RSUEntry;
  usTax: number;
  canadaTax: number;
}

export function FTCOptimizer({ rsuEntry, usTax, canadaTax }: FTCOptimizerProps)
```

2. Component sections:
   - **Before FTC**: Horizontal stacked bar showing US tax + Canada tax = total double taxation
   - **After FTC**: Horizontal bar showing reduced total with FTC applied
   - **Savings highlight**: Green badge with dollar amount and percentage saved
   - **Strategy card**: Recommendation ("File US first, claim Canadian tax credit") with Article XV reference
   - **Explanation accordion**: Collapsible section explaining treaty benefits

3. Visual design:
   - Use `recharts` BarChart with custom colors: US tax (blue-600), Canada tax (red-600), FTC savings (green-500)
   - Responsive: mobile stacked vertical, desktop horizontal comparison
   - TailwindCSS classes matching existing fintech aesthetic (rounded-xl, shadow-lg, gradient backgrounds)

4. Integration in `/app/rsu/[id]/page.tsx`:
   - Import FTCOptimizer below existing TaxComparison component
   - Pass calculated US/Canada tax values from existing calculation
   - Wrap in section with heading "Foreign Tax Credit Analysis"

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- FTC optimizer renders on RSU detail page (/rsu/[id])
- Visual comparison clearly shows tax with/without FTC (bars scale correctly)
- Savings amount matches FTC calculation (within $1)
- Filing recommendation displays correct strategy based on optimal FTC
- Component responsive on mobile (375px) and desktop (1920px)
- Article XV explanation text included and accessible

childTasks.title: Implement PDF Tax Summary Export with jsPDF
childTasks.description:
**Files to create:**
- `/app/api/export/[id]/route.ts` - PDF generation API endpoint
- `/lib/pdf/tax-summary-generator.ts` - PDF document builder
- `/components/export-button.tsx` - Reusable export button component

**Files to modify:**
- `/app/page.tsx` - Add export button to dashboard RSU list
- `/app/rsu/[id]/page.tsx` - Add export button to detail page header

**Dependencies:**

npm install jspdf jspdf-autotable
npm install --save-dev @types/jspdf @types/jspdf-autotable
```

**Implementation:**

import { generateTaxSummaryPDF } from '@/lib/pdf/tax-summary-generator';

  { params }: { params: { id: string } }
) {
  // Fetch RSU entry from SQLite
  // Generate PDF buffer

}
```

2. Create `/lib/pdf/tax-summary-generator.ts`:
   - Use jsPDF A4 portrait format
   - Header: "TaxBridge Tax Summary" with logo placeholder
   - Section 1: RSU Details (date, employer, shares, FMV, total value in USD/CAD)
   - Section 2: Dual-Country Tax Breakdown (table with US federal, state, Canada federal, provincial)
   - Section 3: FTC Calculation (visual breakdown, savings, optimal strategy)
   - Section 4: Net Tax Liability (total tax after FTC)
   - Section 5: Required Forms Checklist (W-2, 1040/1040-NR, T1, T4, FBAR, 8938, 8833 with checkboxes)
   - Footer: Generated date, "This is not tax advice" disclaimer
   - Use jspdf-autotable for tabular data
   - Color scheme: Professional blue (#1e40af) headers, clean typography (Helvetica)

3. Create `/components/export-button.tsx`:
   - Download icon + "Export PDF" text
   - onClick: fetch `/api/export/[id]`, trigger browser download
   - Loading state during PDF generation
   - TailwindCSS: bg-blue-600 hover:bg-blue-700 transition

4. Add button to:
   - Dashboard: Each RSU row gets small export icon button
   - Detail page: Primary action button in header next to "Edit"

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Clicking export button downloads PDF named `taxbridge-summary-{rsuId}.pdf`
- PDF contains all 5 sections with correct data from database
- FTC calculation in PDF matches UI display (within $1)
- Forms checklist includes all 7 required forms
- PDF renders cleanly on mobile and desktop browsers
- Loading spinner shows during generation (typical <2s)
- PDF styling matches TaxBridge brand (modern, professional, fintech aesthetic)
